description: Adds an in-app alerts center accessible from the Harness UI so users can see important events in the product itself.
Enables the AI Engineering Insights experience in AI DLC Insights, replacing the existing AI Insights experience with enhanced AI capabilities and a redesigned interface.
Enforces RBAC permissions on GitX Webhook APIs, ensuring that only users with the appropriate roles can trigger or manage webhooks.

Enables Rerun with Original Pipeline YAML: when the feature flag and the allow_original_yaml_on_rerun setting are on, a rerun can use the pipeline definition and inputs stored from the original execution (including chained child pipelines), instead of the current Pipeline in Harness or Git. Without the feature flag, that path is blocked and reruns always use the latest Pipeline YAML, even if the Pipeline has changed since the original run.
Redesigned STO Issues page to provide a comprehensive security posture view with trend analysis to surface active and remediated issue trends across Targets within a project.
Enhances Jira ticket metadata by including additional security issue details such as severity, CVSS scores, vulnerability paths, and remediation guidance to improve ticket triage and prioritization.

Enables Jira ticket creation for security issues detected in non-baseline scans, allowing teams to track and remediate vulnerabilities from ad-hoc or on-demand security scans beyond the established baseline.
Migrates all existing IACM cloud resources from the Terraform state file into the new resources table.

Routes pipeline data-ingestion events (step, stage, and pipeline execution summaries) to the self-hosted (General) Kafka cluster instead of the Confluent-managed cluster, completing the Confluent to self-hosted dual-cluster migration.

Account administrators can temporarily act as another user (30-minute session, fully audit-logged, with restricted-action blocklist) to troubleshoot permissions and validate configurations on behalf of users in their account.

Routes pipeline events to the platform Centralised Notification Service (CNS), allowing users to manage pipeline notification rules from a unified notification center instead of only per-pipeline notification rules.
Enables a Dynamic Stage in pipelines, allowing stages to be dynamically injected and executed at runtime using inline YAML or Git-stored definitions.
Enables ServiceNow integration as a data source for change requests and incidents on the Incident Management tab in Team Settings.
Allows roles created at a higher scope (account/org) to be reused in lower scopes.
Enables GitLab tag event triggers for pipelines.
Enables Terraform policy evaluation in Continuous Delivery workflows.
Optimizes Tanzu Application Service (TAS) login performance and reliability by reducing the number of CLI initialisation calls.
Enables OIDC authentication for Azure connectors.
Fixes authentication issues when listing Google Cloud Platform projects.
Enforces Git-based experience for CD entities such as service, environment, infrastructure and override configurations.
Provides detailed logging for Kubernetes operations in CD.
Enforces certain validation for infrastructure definitions in CD.
Internal optimization to use secondary database nodes for TimescaleDB queries to improve performance.
Enables Event Relay triggers for pipelines.
Implements failure strategies for service and infrastructure deployment steps.
Fixes the order of lifecycle hooks in Auto Scaling Group deployments.
Adds rollback support for multi-deployment Auto Scaling Group deployments.
Disables environment API updates for service overrides on overrides V2.
Masks secrets in Kubernetes deployment wrap-up logs for security.
Improves service dashboard load time by optimizing API calls and relocating certain data to enhance performance.
Adds ECS tag support for AWS Lambda deployments.
Enables Open Policy Agent evaluation on runtime context for services.
Fixes a rare bug in how service overrides were merged.
Enables monitoring of task status in ECS deployments.
Enables blue-green deployment validation with same target groups in ECS.
Enables traffic shifting without creating temporary versions.
Enables the use of selectors in asynchronous executable operations.
Enables creating environments groups containing environments from different scopes.
Enables service and environment dashboard at account and organization levels for better visibility.
Fixes a bug where the Delegate selector precedence was not being honored for container step groups.
Fixes a bug where run-as-user and privileged mode settings were not being honored for container step groups.
Implements failure strategies for initialization steps.
Allows users to configure how the Pipeline should handle failures during multi-service/multi-environment/multi-infrastructure deployments.
Enables specification of infrastructure configurations in deployments.
Enables shell script execution host capability checks.
Enables WinRM configuration file copying with proper encoding support.
Allows skipping infrastructure validation during deployments.
Uses Kubernetes API for steady state checks instead of kubectl.
Enables a new landing overview page interface for better visibility and performance.
Ensures sensitive information is kept safe when pulling container images.
Improve handling of istio traffic routing resources for Kuberentes canary deployments
It allows to clone the environment across scopes.
Enable the Notifications management feature at all scopes
Child entity should be resolved by parent git context.
Optimisations for resolving templates for pipeline chaining validation
Feature Flag to enable User marked pipeline failure
Optimisation for ExpressionV2 engine
Allow expression resolution of previous stage in pipeline rollback mode
FF to avoid recreation of resource restarint instance when a queue step is retried
FF to enable to limit subGraph API to only step group nodes
FF to redirect invalid template yaml and input set yaml to yaml view of templates and input set
FF to enable resolution to null value if unresolved expressions come via StringReplacer
FF to enable expression resolution for executionInput
FF to separate out executor services per module for plan creation
FF to reverse the order of stages to execute in the post-execution rollback
FF to mark the parent pipeline status waiting as well, In case child pipeline status is waiting
FF to cache the old retried step group graph in cacheEntities collection
FF to store start and end time of previously skipped nodes in retried execution
FF to migrate the instance expressions resolution to pipeline-service
FF to move expression resolution of kubernetes release functor to pipeline-service
FF to remove grpc call for sweeping outputs from exported variables functor
Mask secret expressions which are part of trigger payload
Includes changed files in push event in trigger expression via <+trigger.changedFiles>
FF to support pipeline filter deletion on org/project deletion
FF to optimize filepath condition evaluations in triggers by allowing multiple conditions in one single delegate task
FF to use newer APIs for GitX branch listing.
FF to enable Verify Step Template
FF to fetch and display Service Instance Identifiers for Health Sources
FF to support execution time inputs for single service propagation, without looping strategy configured at stage level
This will add popover for settings in the Left navigation
This will add a referenced/unreferenced tag to the templates listing card and list view.
FF to allow creating new Monitored Service Template
FF to use remove ambiance population in nodeExecution collection
FF to prompt users to fill in unresolved input variables and reconfiguring pipeline-level notification rule templates.
This optimization makes barrier steps avoid race conditions and unecessary locks
Added support for flexible templates
FF for UI to show resolved templates in preview template as read only mode.
Use stageExecutionId projection while getting ambiance from nodeExecution
This optimization delegates graph lookup to mongodb on NodeExecutionsCache#findAllChildrenWithStatusInAndWithoutOldRetries
FF to enabled notification when trigger fails
FF to start storing template id and version for pipeline/stage/step per execution
FF to enable running a stage manually
FF to enable bulk reconciliation for remote entities
Added support for Barriers for Chained Pipelines
Added support for Expressions Pipeline Notifications
Included the original failed children in the failed children sweeping output to ensure correct execution of Stage Rollback.
FF to Validate License for Pipeline Chaining Feature
FF to enable CD Devops Essentials restrictions
FF to fix the compare commits API issue on BB onprem
FF to support scheduling cron triggers across multiple timezones
Added Always use the stable version support to Template Bar for all template types
FF to enable 4xx response code on pipeline save when OPA policy fails
FF to enable to not process next node of starting node in the graph
Use users' oauth creds to fetch and commit in git
OpenID Connect (OIDC) support in Google Secret Manager, Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and Google Cloud Key Management Service (Cloud KMS connectors
Custom authentication path for AppRole authentication in HashiCorp Vault connector
Custom Certificate feature in Harness is behind this feature flag
Docker Delegate Upgrader is currently behind this feature flag
FF to use CV_LOG_CLUSTER task type for log clustering instead of LOG_CLUSTER
Enables usage of GSA for writing and reading logs from GCP
Flag to follow delegate selector precedence in GitOps steps
Enable proper severity mapping for Code Smells and Bug Smells from SonarQube instead of using info as the default severity
Enable the Banners feature in Account Settings
Enables google dns by default for Harness CI Hosted Builds.
Enable parallel secret resolution for faster initialization time in CI stages & Container step groups
Enables support to modify subsequent updates to the alias `routingConfig` during alias update
Enables skipping the ASG instance health check before instance refresh
FF to enable GitX Webhooks RBAC permissions
FF to support GitX webhook audit events
Enables GitOps Rollout step and Auto Promote Rollout dropdown in GitOps Sync Step
Enables Harness SAST and SCA scanners in the step palette for security tests.
Enables fetching dataflow information for security issue occurrences to provide better visibility into vulnerability paths for Harness SAST and SCA scanners.
Enables EPSS scoring for CVE-associated security issues, allowing prioritization based on the likelihood of exploitation.
Enables new revamped UI for harness code.
Enable FME steps such as Flag create, update, Flag treatment updates etc.
Enables global queueing for hosted build stages, allowing them to be automatically queued and executed once infrastructure resources become available.
Enables a FME step to configure flag targeting rules.
Enables a FME step to support archival of a feature flag.
Support for policies with FME feature flags and feature flag definitions
Enables granular role-based access control for FME Feature Flag archive and unarchive actions.
Support for Delegate 2.0 for all STO steps running in Ingestion mode on Linux and MacOS.
Enables manual override of security issue severity for more accurate prioritization based on issue criticality, exploit exposure, compensating controls, and production impact.
Traffic will be automatically shifted back to the stable version when a canary deployment is deleted.
Removing the traffic configuration from the canary workload to prevent virtual services from being deleted during rollback.
Enables default Productivity configuration on the Issue Management tab in Team Settings.
Enables Sprint Insights in Harness AIDI.
Enables shared developer identity mapping across multiple integrations on the Developers tab in Team Settings.
Feature to enable file update enhancements for GitOps Update release repo step
Added filters to the Exemptions page to narrow down issues based on Issue Type, Target, Target Type, Severity, and many more.
Simplifies pool YAML configuration in Harness-hosted builds, reducing configuration complexity for build infrastructure setup.
FF to enable secret runtime usage event generation for ng
Prints each Kubernetes resource on a separate line in deployment logs for improved readability.
Adds support for Google Cloud Managed Instance Groups in deployment workflows.
FF to enable ECS scale step
Enables cleanup of invalid perpetual task instances to improve system performance.
Optimizes service dashboard performance by skipping certain deployment types from database queries.
Enable the Dynamic executions for the Pipelines. This will allow running a pipeline-yaml without having to save it by passing the YAML as the payload in pipeline execute API.
Improves Helm chart deployment performance by reusing ChartMuseum server instances for S3-backed repositories.
Enables automatic fallback to cached Artifactory repositories when no artifacts are found in the primary repository.
Enables Artifact Registry (AR) integration for artifact signing and verification steps
Enable dimension filters and service instance selection to support Canary Verification in Dynatrace
The Service and Environment columns have been replaced by an Update Summary column that shows service-to-environment mappings for CD stages and schema-to-instance mappings for Database DevOps stages.
Enables a FME pipeline step to create segments (standard and rule-based) with configurable traffic type, owners, and description.
Enables a FME pipeline step to update segment metadata such as description and owners.
Enables a FME pipeline step to delete segments via Harness pipelines.
Enables a FME pipeline step to create flagsets for organizing feature flags.
Enables a FME pipeline step to delete flagsets via Harness pipelines.
Enables a FME pipeline step to add or remove flags from flagsets.
Enables a FME pipeline step to add or remove target keys from segments in a specific environment.
Enables a FME pipeline step to configure targeting rules on rule-based segments.
Enables a FME pipeline step to enable or disable impression tracking on feature flags.
Enables configuration of automated pull request workflows in SCS, allowing teams to define and manage Auto PR behavior for remediation and policy-driven updates.
Controls visibility of navigation options and enables manual pull request (PR) remediation workflows in Supply Chain Security.
Enables users to import OPA policies directly from Git.
Enables SLSA provenance generation for non-containerized artifacts like Helm charts, manifest files, JARs, WARs, etc.
Pipeline-driven data retention
Delegate compatibility for Dynatrace Grail logs health source
Delegate compatibility for CloudWatch Logs health source
Verify step I/O variables in CV config handling
Allows users to mark a pipeline as failed even when no steps are actively running, useful for handling stuck or edge-case executions.
Sends notifications when a pipeline resumes after user approval, manual input, or intervention actions.
Improves pipeline execution performance by optimizing how barrier and config-file steps handle completed callbacks.
Mask Terraform sensitive step outputs
Enables keyless signing and verification for artifacts, SBOMs, and SLSA provenance using identity-based trust instead of long-lived signing keys.
Enables Input Sets filter in execution listing page.
Custom data collection duration for verify step
Persist MS created during template verify step
Marks CV configs as demo in template flows
Enables AI-generated summaries across insights in Harness AIDI, providing high-level, contextual explanations of metric trends and patterns.
Activates AI summaries specifically for efficiency-related insights (DORA only) helping users quickly understand bottlenecks and optimization opportunities.
Enables AI-driven summaries for productivity metrics, highlighting developer output trends and changes over time.
Provides AI summaries for Business Alignment insights, translating engineering metrics into business impact and R&D alignment.
Enables agentic AI-generated summaries that go beyond static explanations by incorporating contextual reasoning and dynamic interpretation across multiple signals.
Enables ServiceNow integration in SEI, allowing ingestion and analysis of ITSM data.
Unlocks advanced configuration options for ServiceNow integrations, supporting custom field mappings and extended data control.
Enables ServiceNow as a supported ITSM provider within the Harness AIDI experience.
Introduces optimized team filtering to improve performance when querying org-wide data across large org trees in Harness AIDI.
Enables custom date range selection in Harness AIDI dashboards, allowing users to analyze insights over flexible time windows beyond predefined ranges.
Enable support for evaluating infrastructure and environment context on run
Extends OPA policy support to FME environments, allowing authoring of policies that fire on environment create, update, and delete operations.
Extends OPA policy support to FME segments, allowing authoring of policies that fire on segment create, update, and delete operations.

Extends OPA policy support to FME segment definitions, allowing authoring of policies that fire on segment definition create, update, and delete operations, including targeting rules and target key removals.
Baseline-based path in deployment log analysis
Log feedback behavior in deployment log analysis state flow
Extra validation of service/env/connector refs on MS create/update
Enables license family classification in SBOMs to classify open-source licenses by their family type for better license compliance management.

Enables the SonarQube integration in Harness IDP, allowing auto-discovery of SonarQube projects and enriching the Software Catalog with code quality metrics, security hotspots, quality gate status, and issue analytics.
Enables agentless workflow execution for Airbyte in Harness IDP
Enables the UI to use /v2/entities, which provides an optimized retrieval of entities along with their associated scorecards, while preserving the filtering behavior of the /v1/entities API.
Enables the GitHub Enterprise provider configuration flow in UI, allowing users to configure their GitHub Enterprise Server instance with OAuth credentials for Git sync and repository access.
Enables support in UI to view cpu and memory metrics for pipeline running in Harness cloud
Enables detection of malicious packages as OSS risks within SBOMs
Enables support for onboarding Bitbucket repositories in Repository Security Posture Management (RSPM)
Enables enhanced Git Experience UI features for OPA policies.
Enables direct batch fetch of policy content from Git during evaluation, improving performance and error reporting.
Disables the old AI integration for generating OPA policies.
Enables the new AI chat experience which enhances the rego generation capability integrated with the Harness MCP V2 to automatically create or update OPA policies.
Detects vulnerabilities originating from container base images during STO container scans.
Provides access to the Harness AI-powered support chat experience.
Restricts delegate registration to approved IP addresses or CIDR ranges defined in a Harness allowlist.
Allows public access to Harness pipeline execution views through pipeline and authentication settings.
Adds enhanced SCIM 2.0 compliance, including user name attributes, metadata fields, and group membership responses.
Enables deletion of SCIM-provisioned users in NextGen while retaining the user in FirstGen.
Automatically accepts SAML user invitations and sends invite notification emails.
Disables email notifications for SAML-based user invitations.
Allows Harness delegates to limit concurrent task acquisition and queue excess tasks based on configured capacity.
Captures Harness delegate agent metrics, including task execution, connectivity, and resource utilization, for immutable delegates.
Enables versioning of CI YAML schemas to support gradual rollout of new schema versions and maintain backward compatibility.
Enables TTY logs for CI builds, providing enhanced log formatting and readability for build output.
Enables TTY logs specifically for Harness-hosted CI builds, improving log formatting and readability for builds running on Harness infrastructure.
Enables the use of pipeline-level variables within CI steps for more flexible and dynamic pipeline configurations.
Enables running CI steps as non-root users in containerless execution environments for enhanced security and compliance.
Enables indirect log upload for CI builds, allowing logs to be uploaded asynchronously to improve build performance and reliability.
Enables support for running CI builds on bare metal infrastructure for improved performance and resource utilization.
Enables support for running CI builds on bare metal infrastructure for free accounts, providing enhanced performance and resource utilization for users on the free tier.

Enables support for running CI builds on bare metal infrastructure for standard accounts with up to 32 concurrent builds, providing enhanced performance and resource utilization for users on the standard tier.
Enables support for running CI builds on Harness-hosted infrastructure for improved performance and simplified setup.
Enables support for running CI builds on Harness-hosted infrastructure using the Runner execution engine for improved performance and reliability.
Disables the older DLite execution engine for CI builds running on Harness-hosted infrastructure, fully transitioning to the newer Runner engine for improved performance and reliability.
Enables support for running CI builds on Harness-hosted virtual machine infrastructure for improved performance and simplified setup.
Enables support for running CI builds on Harness-hosted virtual machine infrastructure with MacOS for improved performance and simplified setup for MacOS builds.
Enables support for extra addon resources in CI builds, allowing users to specify additional resources for CI build execution.
Disables the use of delegate selectors at the CI stage level, enforcing delegate selection at the step level for more granular control over CI build execution.
Enables support for running CI builds on Kubernetes infrastructure for improved scalability and resource utilization.
Enables parsing of savings information from CI build execution for improved cost visibility and optimization.
Enables the new anomalies experience in Cloud & AI Cost Management, providing improved anomaly detection and analysis capabilities.
Enables conditional drill-down for anomalies in Cloud & AI Cost Management, allowing users to view detailed anomaly information based on specific conditions.
Enables lookback recomputation for anomalies in Cloud & AI Cost Management, allowing users to revise existing anomalies with the latest cost data.
Enables extended rerun days for anomalies in Cloud & AI Cost Management, allowing users to rerun anomaly detection on past 15 days of data.
Enables anomaly whitelisting in Cloud & AI Cost Management, allowing users to ignore specific anomalies based on defined rules.
Enables bypassing of additional non-cost category filters for anomalies in Cloud & AI Cost Management, allowing users to focus on cost category anomalies in perspectives.
Enables Prophet V2 anomaly detection in Cloud & AI Cost Management, providing improved anomaly detection capabilities.
Enables the requirement of both absolute and percentage thresholds for anomaly detection in Cloud & AI Cost Management, providing more precise anomaly identification.
Enables AWS passthrough for recommendations in Cloud & AI Cost Management, allowing users to view recommendations directly from AWS without additional processing.
Enables read-only access to AWS passthrough recommendations in Cloud & AI Cost Management, allowing users to view recommendations without making changes.
Enables Node Pool Recommendations V2 in Cloud & AI Cost Management, providing improved recommendation capabilities.
Enables filtering of autoscaled node pools in Cloud & AI Cost Management, allowing users to focus on specific node pool types.
Enables global and per-feature cost type settings for display of recommendations
Use net amortized cost for pass-through recommendations from AWS Cost Explorer
Enable read access to new AWS Passthrough V2 recommendation types (ASG, EBS, RDS)
Read Azure recommendations from the new data store for the frontend
Ingest Azure recommendations into the new data store with updated resource type and monthly cost
Enable auto-inference for K8s workload recommendation application by detecting resource changes
Enable auto-inference for ECS recommendation application by detecting resource changes
Enable cost type settings for Azure passthrough recommendations
Enable cost type settings for node pool recommendations
Display actual realized savings for recommendations
Show recommendations within Perspectives
Enable cost type settings for ECS recommendations
Enables Workload Recommendations V2 in Cloud & AI Cost Management, providing improved recommendation capabilities.
Disables the legacy statistical anomaly detection model and uses only the improved ML-based detection for more accurate anomaly identification.
Enables anomaly detection for each cost type (unblended, amortized, net amortized) in addition to existing anomaly calculations.
Enables viewing and filtering anomalies by cost type (unblended, amortized, net amortized) in Cloud & AI Cost Management.
Enables anomaly detection based on cost categories, allowing organizations to track spending anomalies at the business unit or service level.
Improves cost report query performance and data consistency across Cloud & AI Cost Management dashboards.
Excludes cloud marketplace charges from anomaly detection to focus on infrastructure and service spend anomalies.
Enables preset support for AWS Cost Optimization Hub recommendations, providing predefined recommendation filters and configurations.
Enables Lifecycle Rules in Artifact Registry, allowing users to define and manage lifecycle policies for artifacts.
Enables the Definition Instruction step in Harness pipelines, allowing users to define and manage feature flag definitions in a pipeline step.
Enables the Spark-based ETL processor to optimize and accelerate data processing performance for the Azure DevOps Boards integration.
Enables an early-access version of the redesigned Harness UI.
Enables the redesigned side navigation in Harness, providing a refreshed layout and improved discoverability of modules and settings.
Enables the redesigned Connectors experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing connectors.
Enables the redesigned CCM Connectors experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing CCM connectors.
Enables the redesigned Pipelines List experience in Harness, providing a refreshed layout and improved discoverability of pipelines.
Enables the redesigned secrets management experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing secrets.
Enables the redesigned Pipelines experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing pipelines.
Enables the redesigned Delegate V2 experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing delegates.
Enables the redesigned Delegate UI experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing delegates.
Enables the redesigned Pipeline Settings experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing pipeline settings.
Enables the redesigned home page experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for the main dashboard.
Enables the redesigned Vulnerabilities experience in Harness, providing an updated interface for viewing and triaging security vulnerabilities.
Enables the redesigned Executions experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing executions.
Enables the redesigned Environments experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing environments.
Enables the redesigned Execution SSCA experience in Harness, providing a refreshed interface for managing software supply chain assurance.
